title: Intensely Fermented Greek Lamb Pasta
description: A crumbly and romantic Greek dish featuring linguine pasta and beurre-monte, made with fermented ingredients for a unique flavor, and low-glycemic-friendly for a healthier option.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook the linguine pasta according to the package instructions until al dente.
In a large cazuela, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the fermented lamb shoulder and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the kalamata olives, artichoke hearts, and cooked pasta to the cazuela, stirring to combine.
In a small bowl, whisk together the beurre-monte and lemon juice, then pour the mixture over the pasta and lamb.
Sprinkle the crumbly feta cheese and chopped parsley over the top of the pasta.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the cazuela to the preheated oven and bake for 15 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the cazuela from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
